behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction specifies all key file paths (chart/values.yaml, chart/values.schema.json, chart/templates/NOTES.txt, chart/newsfragments/62048.significant.rst), exact literal strings (deprecation comment text, NOTES.txt warning text), schema keys (volumeClaimTemplates under workers.celery), required example keys (name, storageClassName, accessModes, resources), field names (enableDefault, persistence, hostAliases, schedulerName), and default values. The test assertions on documentation comments

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes what needs to be added/changed (new field, deprecation comments, schema updates, NOTES.txt warning, newsfragment) but doesn't specify exact line numbers or the precise code/template logic needed for backward compatibility (e.g., how to implement precedence in Helm templates). The developer must figure out the implementation details.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for behavioral outcomes (field existence with correct default, deprecation text presence, schema structure, helm rendering with volume names appearing in output) rather than implementation-specific details. Alternative implementations (different template logic, different comment formatting beyond required strings, different schema descriptions) would pass as long as they produce the correct behavior.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: While some tests do check file contents (deprecation comments, NOTES.txt text), the TestHelmRender class actually executes `helm template` as a subprocess and verifies rendered output contains expected volume names. This tests actual behavior - that the chart renders correctly with old, new, and both value locations.

test_not_tautological.reason: The tests require actual changes: adding a new field to values.yaml (test_celery_volumeclaimtemplates_exists), adding schema entries (test_celery_volumeclaimtemplates_in_schema), modifying template logic so helm renders volumeClaimTemplates from the new location (test_new_volumeclaimtemplates_location_works), and ensuring backward compatibility (test_backward_compatibility). A stub/no-op would fail these tests since the fields don't exist in the base repo.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/airflow and solve.sh contains the complete gold patch. An agent could read solution/solve.sh (if accessible in the container) or simply apply the patch. The Dockerfile doesn't remove or protect the solution directory. Additionally, the test file itself is at a predictable location and could be read to reverse-engineer exact requirements.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All explicit requirements from instruction.md are tested: new field in values.yaml with empty list default, deprecation comment on old field, schema updates (type, description, examples with required keys, deprecated description on old field), NOTES.txt deprecation warning, backward compatibility (old location works, new location works, new takes precedence), and the newsfragment file existence (test_newsfragment_exists in truncated tests).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized into descriptive classes (TestValuesYaml, TestValuesSchema, TestNotesTxt, TestHelmRender, TestYamlStructure, TestCommentsAndDocumentation) with clear method names and docstrings explaining what each test verifies.

structured_data_schema.reason: The instruction specifies the exact JSON schema structure needed: type 'array', description field, at least 2 examples with specific keys (name, storageClassName, accessModes, resources). The values.yaml structure is also documented (empty list default, placement under workers.celery).

no_typos.reason: All file paths (chart/values.yaml, chart/values.schema.json, chart/templates/NOTES.txt, chart/newsfragments/62048.significant.rst), field names (volumeClaimTemplates, enableDefault, persistence, hostAliases, schedulerName), and commands are correct with no typos.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction describes what to add and where (which sections, which files) but doesn't specify exact line numbers or the precise template logic. The file locations are natural for the task domain (Helm chart structure). Mentioning existing fields in workers.celery is necessary context, not implementation leakage.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The gold patch makes localized changes to 4 files (values.yaml, values.schema.json, NOTES.txt, new newsfragment file) with targeted additions/modifications rather than full-file rewrites, and these changes are consistent with the instruction requirements.
